sections.instruction: Choose the correct letter A, B or C.

questions.content: Why has James chosen to do a case study on the company Furniture Rossi?
options: A It has enjoyed global success.
B It is still in a developmental phase.
C It is an example of a foreign company being rebranded for Australia.

questions.content: According to James, why did Luca Rossi start a furniture company?
options: A Furniture-making was already a family occupation.
B Rossi saw a need for hand-crafted furniture.
C The work Rossi had done previously was unrewarding.

questions.content: What gave Furniture Rossi a competitive advantage over other furniture companies?
What does the tutor recommend James does when writing the second draft of his case study?
What do the tutor and James agree was wrong with James’ last presentation?
options: B It focused too much on statistics.
C There was not enough interaction with the audience.

sections.instruction: Complete the flow chart below. Choose FIVE answers from the list below and write the correct letter A-G next to questions 26-30.
Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context:
ROCK ART
Why rock art is important to researchers
It provides evidence about
• Evolution
• {{BLANK}}

Global similarities in rock art
• Humans often had large {{BLANK}}
• Animals were common but a {{BLANK}} was always drawn from the side or from above.
• Unlikely that contact through {{BLANK}} resulted in similar artistic styles

Why our ancestors produced rock art
Research suggests rock art was produced
• Firstly for reasons of {{BLANK}}
• Later for social, spiritual and {{BLANK}} reasons.

questions.content: Animals were common but a {{BLANK}} was always drawn from the side or from above.
